sessioncheck – Agent Instructions

Source: AGENTS.md

Project Overview

sessioncheck is a lightweight R package that provides tools for checking whether an R session is in a “clean” state. It is intended to be called at the top of a script as a drop-in replacement for the common (but misleading) idiom of rm(list = ls()). Rather than silently wiping the environment, it surfaces problems so the user can make informed decisions.

  • Author: Danielle Navarro
  • License: MIT
  • Lifecycle: Experimental
  • Dependencies: Zero — base R only (no Imports in DESCRIPTION)
  • Website: https://sessioncheck.djnavarro.net/

Repository Layout

R/
  api.R          # Public-facing exported functions (the main user surface)
  class.R        # S3 class constructors and methods
  internal.R     # Internal helpers: validators (.validate_*) and checkers (.get_*_status)
  utils.R        # Package initialization (.onLoad), session snapshot, small helpers

tests/testthat/
  test-api.R
  test-class.R
  test-sessioncheckers.R
  test-validators.R
  test-other.R
  _snaps/        # Snapshot files (auto-generated by testthat)

vignettes/articles/   # Five long-form articles rendered via pkgdown
man/                  # Auto-generated by roxygen2 — never edit directly

Naming & Code Conventions

  • Private functions are prefixed with . (e.g., .get_package_status(), .validate_action()).
  • Public functions are exported and have no prefix.
  • All parameters use snake_case.
  • S3 class names follow the pattern sessioncheck_<noun> (e.g., sessioncheck_status, sessioncheck_sessioncheck).
  • Internal helpers live in internal.R; package-level utilities and .onLoad live in utils.R.

Public API

The eight exported check functions share a consistent interface:

check_*(action = "warn", allow_*/required_*/max_* = NULL)
  • action: one of "error", "warn", "message", "none".
  • The second argument is check-specific (an allowlist, a required-values list, or a numeric threshold).
  • All return an invisible S3 status object.

sessioncheck() is the top-level orchestrator; it delegates to individual check_* functions and accepts their arguments via ....

Configuration also flows from options(sessioncheck = list(...)), which is merged with explicit arguments. The precedence is:

explicit arg > options(sessioncheck = ...) > function default > hard-coded allowlist

S3 Classes

Two classes are defined in class.R:

Class Description
sessioncheck_status Result of a single check. Contains a named logical vector (status) and a type string. TRUE means a problem was detected.
sessioncheck_sessioncheck Composite result from sessioncheck(). A list of sessioncheck_status objects.

Both classes have format, print, and as.data.frame methods.

Hard-coded Allowlists (do not change without care)

Several things are always allowed regardless of user configuration:

  • Packages with "base" priority on the search path (they cannot be safely detached).
  • The sessioncheck namespace itself (always loaded while checks run).
  • Dot-prefixed objects in .GlobalEnv (e.g., .Random.seed).
  • IDE-injected environments ("tools:rstudio", "tools:positron", "Autoloads").

Skills

When asked to add a new check_*() function, load the add-check-function skill. It contains a step-by-step recipe covering which files to change, in what order, with code templates matching the existing patterns.

Do Not Touch Without Explicit Instruction

Auto-generated files — never edit directly

  • man/*.Rd — generated by roxygen2 via devtools::document(). Edit the roxygen2 comments in the R/ source files instead.
  • NAMESPACE — also generated by roxygen2. Never edit by hand.
  • tests/testthat/_snaps/ — snapshot files are generated by testthat. Only update them after deliberately reviewing the diff with testthat::snapshot_review(); do not edit the .md files directly.

Public API — no changes without explicit approval

  • Exported function names — renaming or removing any exported function is a breaking change for users.
  • Function signatures — do not add, remove, rename, or reorder parameters of any check_*() function or sessioncheck(). Default values count: changing action = "warn" to any other default is a breaking change.
  • S3 class structure — do not change the fields of sessioncheck_status or sessioncheck_sessioncheck, and do not add or remove S3 methods, without explicit instruction. These classes are part of the user-visible contract.

Dependencies — the zero-import rule is strict

  • Do not add any package to Imports in DESCRIPTION for any reason. If a task seems to require an external package, stop and raise it with the user instead.
  • Suggests additions (e.g., for a new test helper) are acceptable but should still be flagged before adding.

Hard-coded allowlists — safety-critical

  • Do not modify the logic in internal.R that unconditionally allows base-priority packages, the sessioncheck namespace, dot-prefixed globals, or IDE-injected environments. These rules exist to prevent the package from producing false positives or breaking in normal R sessions. Changes here require explicit discussion.

Opportunistic refactoring — don’t

  • Do not refactor, reformat, or reorganize code in files beyond the scope of the task at hand, even if it looks like it could be cleaner. Keeping diffs small and focused makes review much easier.

Development Workflow

This package uses standard R package development tools:

# Load package for interactive development
devtools::load_all()

# Run full test suite
devtools::test()

# Run a specific test file
testthat::test_file("tests/testthat/test-api.R")

# Check the package (equivalent of R CMD check)
devtools::check()

# Regenerate documentation from roxygen2 comments
devtools::document()

# Build and preview pkgdown site
pkgdown::build_site()

Snapshot tests live in tests/testthat/_snaps/. Update them with testthat::snapshot_review() or devtools::test() after confirming changes are intentional.

Git and GitHub

Always ask the user for confirmation before making a git commit or git push, and before opening or modifying a pull request. Prepare the changes (edit files, run tests, stage with git add) and then pause to show the user what will be committed and ask whether to proceed. Do not commit or push autonomously, even when a task description implies it or the changes are small.

Testing Patterns

  • Framework: testthat edition 3.
  • Mocking: testthat::local_mocked_bindings() is used to isolate checks from actual session state. Use the same approach when adding tests for new checks.
  • New public functions should have tests in the appropriate test-*.R file covering: normal operation, edge cases, and the effect of each action value.
  • Validators are tested separately in test-validators.R.

Documentation

  • All exported functions and S3 methods are documented with roxygen2 in the same .R file as their definition.
  • The man/ directory is auto-generated — never edit .Rd files directly.
  • Vignettes are .Rmd files under vignettes/articles/ and are rendered as pkgdown articles (not CRAN vignettes).
  • After editing roxygen2 comments, run devtools::document() before committing.

Style Preferences

  • Use US spelling in all code comments and documentation: e.g., behavior (not behaviour), itemize (not itemise), modeling (not modelling), color (not colour), analyze (not analyse).

Design Principles to Preserve

  1. Zero external dependencies. Do not add any packages to Imports. The package must work with base R alone.
  2. Non-destructive by default. Checks warn rather than error by default. The user always retains control.
  3. Transparent allowlisting. Legitimate session state can always be whitelisted; nothing is silently ignored.
  4. Consistent function signatures. New check functions should follow the existing check_*(action, allow_*/required_*/max_*) pattern and return an invisible sessioncheck_status object created with new_status().
  5. Early validation. Argument validation happens at the top of each function via .validate_*() helpers before any session inspection occurs.

On this page

Developed by Danielle Navarro

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
